varieties.name: White Quinoa (Generic Commercial)
varieties.traits: Cream to golden seeds, 1.5-2mm diameter, light fluffy texture when cooked
varieties.flesh_fruit: Mild, nutty flavor; soft texture; versatile in cooking
varieties.yield_notes: Low to moderate in humid subtropical climates (500-1000 kg/ha estimated)
varieties.growing_notes: Most commonly available in Australia. Winter planting (April-June) only. Requires night temps below 20°C for germination. Sensitive to humid subtropical climates summer heat (>32°C) and humidity. High risk of fungal diseases in humid conditions. Saponin layer helps protect against pests but requires thorough washing.
varieties.availability: The Seed Vine (humid subtropical climates area), Boondie Seeds, Australian Wheatgrass (organic), Lost Seed

varieties.name: Red Quinoa
varieties.traits: Red-brown seeds, retains shape well, slightly chewy texture, colorful
varieties.flesh_fruit: Nutty, earthy, robust flavor; firm texture maintains in salads
varieties.yield_notes: Low to moderate (similar to white quinoa in humid subtropical climates)
varieties.growing_notes: Marginally better heat tolerance than white. Best for autumn planting (March-May). Seed coat thickness helps resist humidity-related degradation. Still struggles above 30°C. Good for humid subtropical climates winter but avoid flowering during hot spells.
varieties.availability: Royal Nut Company (organic), specialty seed suppliers

varieties.name: Black Quinoa
varieties.traits: Black to deep purple seeds, smallest size, crunchiest texture, high fiber
varieties.flesh_fruit: Earthy, slightly sweet; firmest texture of all quinoas
varieties.yield_notes: Lower than other colors (500-800 kg/ha estimated)
varieties.growing_notes: Longest maturity time (110-130 days). Most heat-tolerant of the three colors but still challenged by humid subtropical climates climate. Slower germination. Best autumn planting. Thick seed coat somewhat protects against humidity.
varieties.availability: Limited; occasionally from specialty Australian seed companies

varieties.name: Rainbow/Tri-Color Quinoa
varieties.traits: Blend of white, red, and black seeds; visually appealing mix
varieties.flesh_fruit: Varied flavors and textures in one dish
varieties.growing_notes: Popular consumer product. humid subtropical climates challenges same as individual colors. Winter growing only. Provides genetic diversity which may help with adaptation.
varieties.availability: Australian health food suppliers, some seed companies

varieties.name: BEW / Kruso White
varieties.origin: Selected in Kununurra, Western Australia; released 2019
varieties.traits: White seeds, compact seed heads, late maturing, Australian-adapted
varieties.yield_notes: Up to 2-3 t/ha in ideal irrigated tropical conditions (Kununurra)

varieties.name: Titicaca
varieties.traits: White seeds, early maturity (90-110 days), uniform, photoperiod-insensitive
varieties.flesh_fruit: High-quality white grain, fluffy when cooked
varieties.yield_notes: 2-3 t/ha in temperate conditions; poor in humid subtropical climates

varieties.name: Puno
varieties.traits: White to cream seeds, early maturing, high saponin content (bitter)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Requires thorough washing to remove bitterness
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to high in appropriate climates

varieties.name: Chilean Coastal/Lowland Types
varieties.origin: Coastal Chile (sea level to 800m), 3000+ years cultivation
varieties.traits: Smaller seeds (<2mm), photoperiod-insensitive, adapted to moderate coastal climates
varieties.flesh_fruit: Variable quality depending on specific landrace

varieties.name: Regalona (Chilean)
varieties.traits: White seeds, coastal adaptation, heat-tolerant (relatively)
varieties.growing_notes: Developed for temperate coastal conditions. Better heat tolerance than highland types but still struggles above 30°C. Photoperiod-insensitive. Potential for humid subtropical climates winter trials. Requires cool nights for best results.
varieties.availability: International suppliers; not common in Australia

varieties.name: White Quinoa (Generic)
varieties.traits: Standard white/cream seeds
varieties.yield_notes: Near zero expected in tropical monsoon climates climate
varieties.growing_notes: THEORETICAL ONLY. Would require complete climate modification (greenhouse with cooling, dehumidification). Not viable for tropical monsoon climates outdoor cultivation. Seeds available but success rate essentially 0% in typical tropical monsoon climates conditions.

varieties.name: Him Shakti
varieties.traits: High-yielding Indian variety for mountain climates
varieties.flesh_fruit: Developed for Indian conditions but COOL climates
varieties.yield_notes: Good in Himalayas; unsuitable for tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: Chilean Coastal Lowland Varieties
varieties.traits: Sea-level adaptation, some heat tolerance
varieties.yield_notes: Unknown in tropical monsoon climates; likely very poor
varieties.growing_notes: MOST HEAT-TOLERANT quinoa types globally. Evolved in Chilean Mediterranean coast (cool winters, warm dry summers). Still requires seasonal cooling and LOW humidity. Tropical monsoon year-round heat and monsoon humidity would likely prevent success. Worth experimental trial in Tropical monsoon "coolest" season (December-February) but low success probability.

varieties.name: Ecuadorian Varieties
varieties.traits: Closest to tropical adaptation, but HIGH altitude (cool)
varieties.yield_notes: Unknown; extremely low expected in tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: Royal Quinoa (Quinoa Real)
varieties.origin: Southern Bolivian Altiplano (salt flats, 12,000 ft elevation)
varieties.traits: Largest seeds (2-3mm), pearly white, premium quality, 50+ local varieties
varieties.flesh_fruit: Nutty, fluffy, superior flavor and texture
varieties.growing_notes: Most prized quinoa globally. Grows ONLY in extreme high-altitude Bolivian salt deserts. Requires freezing nights, hot days, low humidity, saline soil. Completely incompatible with humid subtropical climates or tropical monsoon climates. "Champagne of Quinoa".
varieties.availability: Imported grain available; seed cultivation impossible outside Altiplano

varieties.name: Pasankalla (INIA 415)
varieties.traits: Dark red-brown to purple seeds, traditional Andean variety, high protein (14%+)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Nutty, rich flavor; used in traditional Andean dishes
varieties.growing_notes: Ancient heirloom. Sacred to Andean cultures. High-altitude adapted (3500-3900m). Heat-sensitive. Cultural significance in Peru. Not viable for low-altitude tropical/subtropical regions.

varieties.name: Chullpi
varieties.traits: Translucent white seeds, sweet milky flavor when fresh, rare variety
varieties.flesh_fruit: Uniquely sweet, delicate; prized for traditional Andean soups
varieties.growing_notes: Rare heirloom variety at risk of extinction. Cold-hardy, requires high altitude. Sweet flavor without bitterness. Cultural importance in traditional Andean cuisine. Not viable outside high-altitude cool climates.
varieties.availability: Extremely limited; Andean seed conservation programs

varieties.name: Kancolla (Blanca de Juli)
varieties.traits: White seeds, traditional lakeside variety
varieties.growing_notes: Traditional variety from favorable Titicaca microclimate. Requires cool temperatures, moderate moisture. High-altitude crop. Not adapted to tropical lowlands.

varieties.name: Salcedo INIA
varieties.traits: White seeds, improved variety, low saponin, disease resistant
varieties.growing_notes: One of Peru's most successful improved varieties. Bred for Altiplano (3800-3900m elevation). Tolerates frost and drought of high Andes. NOT heat-tolerant. Unsuitable for tropical lowlands.

varieties.name: Negra Collana (INIA 420)
varieties.traits: Black seeds, traditional variety, high antioxidants
varieties.growing_notes: Traditional black quinoa variety. High nutritional value, rich in anthocyanins. High-altitude adapted. Long growing cycle (120+ days). Not suitable for tropical climates.

varieties.name: Kaslala Multicolor
varieties.traits: Multiple seed colors (ivory, red, rose, burgundy, black), landrace blend
varieties.growing_notes: Traditional multicolor heirloom landrace. Genetic richness represents millennia of Andean farmer selection. Requires cool climate. Introduced to Colorado 1980s. Not adapted to tropical/subtropical lowlands.
varieties.availability: Specialty heirloom seed companies (USA); Ujamaa Seeds

varieties.name: Amarilla Sacaca (INIA 427)
varieties.traits: Yellow-golden seeds, improved variety
varieties.growing_notes: Peruvian improved variety for Andean valleys (2750-3650m). Frost and drought resistant. Requires cool temperatures. Not viable for tropical monsoon or humid subtropical climates.

varieties.name: Witulla, Wariponcho, Cheweca
varieties.traits: Colored panicles, extremely cold-hardy varieties
varieties.growing_notes: High-altitude specialty varieties adapted to lowest temperatures. Extreme cold tolerance but highly heat-sensitive. Cultural varieties maintained by indigenous communities. Not viable below 3000m elevation.

varieties.name: Huariponcho
varieties.traits: Traditional variety, colored seeds
varieties.growing_notes: Heritage variety used in breeding programs. Excellent frost resistance, disease tolerance. High-altitude crop. At risk variety being conserved through agro-tourism efforts in Peru.
